posts.raw_content:
Department of the Army, Permanent Orders 170-18 (June 19, 2003)

The President of the United States of America, authorized by Act of Congress July 9, 1918 (amended by an act of July 25, 1963), takes pride in presenting the Silver Star (Posthumously) to Private Jerod Rhoton Dennis, United States Army, for conspicuous gallantry and intrepidity in action while serving a Rifleman with Company B, 3d Battalion, 504th Parachute Infantry Regiment, 82d Airborne Division, during combat operations in support of Operation ENDURING FREEDOM, on 25 April 2003, in Afghanistan. Private Dennis was a part of a quick reaction force that was moving to reinforce American soldiers in contact north east of the Shkin Firebase along the Afghanistan-Pakistan border. His actions under overwhelming direct enemy fire were instrumental in the survival of an American casualty and his fellow soldiers. Private Dennis’ actions reflected great credit upon himself, the Devil Brigade, Joint Task Force 82, and the United States Army.

Jerod saved my life that day. I will never forget locking eyes with him and seeing the moment he knew his injuries were mortal and made the choice to go out fighting. He took over an enemy gun truck (.50cal mounted on back of a Toyota Hilux and suppressed the enemy enough to let us break contact and get behind the QRF that came to assist. When we found him later, riddled with bullets including in his femoral artery, he had a grenade clutched in his hands ready for any enemies who tried to take him. 

I owe him the best life I can live and pray every day that I earned his sacrifice.
